macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: UN warns global growth is set to slow as Middle East tensions add economic pressure
items.summary: The UN said the global economy faces slower growth, citing renewed shock from the Middle East crisis and lingering uncertainty around inflation and trade. The warning underscores continued macroeconomic fragility across major regions.

items.title: RBI bulletin flags West Asia uncertainty as a drag on the global economy
items.summary: India’s central bank said the world economy remains shadowed by uncertainty tied to West Asia, while also noting crude oil prices as a risk to the external sector outlook. The comments highlight how energy-market volatility is feeding into inflation and growth concerns.

items.title: Oil prices rise amid slow progress in US-Iran peace talks
items.summary: Crude prices moved higher as investors weighed geopolitical risk and sluggish progress in diplomatic talks. The move adds to inflation pressure and could affect central bank policy expectations if energy costs stay elevated.

items.title: Asian equities open firmer, with Sensex and Nifty rallying in early trade
items.summary: Indian stocks opened higher, with the Sensex up more than 300 points and the Nifty moving above 23,700 as global markets rallied. The move suggests a risk-on tone in regional equities despite broader macro uncertainty.

items.title: G7 finance ministers search for common ground on trade and Russia
items.summary: G7 finance chiefs are trying to align on policy responses to trade imbalances and Russia-related issues, even as divisions remain. The talks matter for markets because they could shape tariffs, sanctions, and broader global trade conditions.

items.title: Chinese commerce minister meets WTO chief amid trade-policy tensions
items.summary: China’s commerce minister met with the WTO director-general as major economies continue to navigate trade frictions and industrial-policy disputes. The meeting comes as policymakers seek to manage global trade risks that could spill over into growth and markets.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Bitcoin holds near $77K after volatile 24-hour swing
items.summary: Bitcoin traded between roughly $74,657 and $77,404 over the past day and was last around $76,962, after earlier dipping below key support levels. Several crypto outlets tied the move to broader risk-off sentiment and heavy liquidations across the market.

items.title: Crypto market reels as Bitcoin slides under $77,000
items.summary: Crypto news aggregators reported that Bitcoin fell under $77,000 amid an oil shock and rising Treasury yields, with some coverage citing a broader selloff and hundreds of millions of dollars in liquidations. The move pressured major altcoins and risk assets across the sector.

items.title: Coinbase explores direct access to Fed payment rails
items.summary: Recent coverage says Coinbase is exploring potential direct access to U.S. Federal Reserve payment rails, a development that could expand the exchange’s role in traditional finance infrastructure. The reporting also highlights ongoing regulatory and policy changes around stablecoins and payment systems.

items.title: Coinbase to launch perpetual-style equity index futures
items.summary: Coinbase’s derivatives arm is set to launch perpetual-style equity index futures on a U.S.-regulated exchange, expanding its product lineup beyond crypto-only offerings. The move signals continued convergence between crypto markets and traditional financial instruments.

items.title: U.S. Strategic Bitcoin Reserve push returns to Congress
items.summary: Brave New Coin reports that discussion of a U.S. Strategic Bitcoin Reserve has returned to Congress as the White House prepares an executive move. If advanced, the proposal could mark a major shift in U.S. digital asset policy.

items.title: Ethereum steadies as bullish post-upgrade momentum continues
items.summary: Ethereum-focused coverage says ETH has maintained upward momentum after the Fusaka upgrade, helping it recover toward the $3,000 area. The narrative centers on improved sentiment and a possible move toward higher resistance levels.

items.title: PENGU token transfers to exchanges spark market speculation
items.summary: CoinMarketCal’s news feed noted large volumes of Pudgy Penguins’ PENGU tokens moving from the project’s deployment address to centralized exchanges. The transfers have raised questions among traders even as the token benefits from a broader market rally.

items.title: Binance lists DePHY Network ahead of airdrop-linked trading
items.summary: CoinMarketCal reported that Binance will list DePHY Network (PHY) and open USDT trading, an announcement that often drives heightened volatility and liquidity. The event is also tied to a PHY airdrop.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Google AI updates highlighted at I/O 2026 sessions
items.summary: Conference talks surfaced several notable Google AI developments, including AI Studio's Build feature, Gemini 3.1 Flash Light, Genie 3, and Gemma 4. The demos emphasized cheaper multimodal inference, local/on-device models, and agentic workflows running without cloud APIs.

items.title: Google memo fuels renewed debate over open-source AI's competitive threat
items.summary: A leaked internal Google memo argued that proprietary AI models may be losing ground to the open-source community, citing faster iteration and lower costs from accessible models such as Meta's LLaMA. The discussion has renewed industry focus on whether model-scale advantages still provide a durable moat.

items.title: ServiceNow CEO discusses AI stacks reshaping enterprise software
items.summary: A recent analysis session focused on how AI stacks are changing enterprise organizations, with attention on observability, data meshes, and faster decision-making loops. The conversation reflected a broader trend toward agentic systems tied to structured enterprise data.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Bungie reportedly planning major layoffs as Destiny 3 is said not to be in active development
items.summary: Multiple gaming outlets are reporting that Bungie may be preparing significant layoffs amid continued changes around Destiny 2, with one report also claiming Destiny 3 is not currently in active production. The news underscores ongoing turbulence at the studio behind Destiny and Marathon.

items.title: BitSummit 2026 announces awards, including VGC’s Media Highlight honor
items.summary: Japan’s BitSummit indie showcase has announced its 2026 awards, recognizing standout games and media coverage from the event. The awards highlight continued momentum for indie development and coverage in the global games scene.

items.title: New action RPG 'Surfpunk' unveiled by developers tied to League of Legends' Ekko
items.summary: A new action RPG called Surfpunk has been announced, developed by creators associated with League of Legends' Ekko. Early coverage suggests it is one of the more notable new project reveals in recent gaming news.

items.title: Warhammer Age of Sigmar: Deathmaster announced
items.summary: A new Warhammer Age of Sigmar title, Deathmaster, has been announced, adding to the expanding slate of Games Workshop-based games. Details remain limited, but the reveal is drawing attention from strategy and fantasy game fans.

items.title: Pokémon GO limited-time event adds new shiny hunt with a major catch
items.summary: Pokémon GO is running a new limited-time event that introduces another shiny target for players, though coverage notes there is an important limitation or catch. The event is among the latest live-service updates in mobile gaming.

items.title: Neverness to Everness version 1.1 update 'Dreamwalk Corridor' launches June 3
items.summary: The open-world title Neverness to Everness is set to receive its 1.1 update, Dreamwalk Corridor, on June 3. The patch announcement is one of the clearest near-term release updates in recent gaming news.

items.title: Lucasfilm confirms Cal Kestis' story continues, fueling Star Wars Jedi 3 speculation
items.summary: Lucasfilm has confirmed that Cal Kestis' adventures will continue, renewing speculation that a third Star Wars Jedi game is on the way. While not a formal game reveal, the comment is a notable franchise development for fans.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Amazon brings Alexa+ to the web, broadening its assistant push
items.summary: Amazon has expanded Alexa+ beyond devices and onto the web, signaling a bigger push into agentic digital assistance and direct competition with other major consumer AI platforms. The move is part of a wider effort to make Alexa more useful across browsing and task automation.

items.title: Samsung unveils Galaxy Z TriFold with redesigned dual-hinge foldable design
items.summary: Samsung’s latest foldable, the Galaxy Z TriFold, features a dual-hinge mechanism, upgraded materials, and a 10-inch display that unfolds from a pocketable frame. The device represents the company’s most advanced foldable hardware yet.

items.title: Google and Qualcomm push Android PCs toward the mainstream
items.summary: Google and Qualcomm are working to bring Android PCs into the mainstream by scaling the mobile operating system to desktop computing. The effort is aimed at challenging Microsoft and Apple in the personal computer market.

items.title: Quantum Motion deploys first full-stack quantum computer built with standard silicon fabrication
items.summary: Quantum Motion says it has deployed the first full-stack quantum computer manufactured using standard silicon chip fabrication techniques. The milestone could help accelerate the path to scalable quantum systems.

items.title: Qualcomm launches Snapdragon W5+ Gen 2 and W5 Gen 2 wearables platforms
items.summary: Qualcomm’s new Snapdragon W5+ Gen 2 and W5 Gen 2 platforms are designed to improve battery life, design flexibility, and connectivity in wearables. The launch targets long-standing barriers to broader smartwatch and wearable adoption.

items.title: Gartner warns organizations to block AI browsers over security risks
items.summary: Gartner is urging enterprises to block AI-powered browsers, warning they can expose sensitive data and weaken existing security controls. The guidance reflects growing concern about new browser-based tools creating enterprise-wide risk.
